setup.title: Setup Needs Attention
setup.description: CCP detected that this environment is not fully configured yet. Confirm the repair steps you want to run.
setup.wrappersDesc: Installing them makes terminal `claude` sessions start through CCP and reuse the current state directory.
setup.manualInstall: CCP could not find a runnable `ccp` binary automatically. Run this in a terminal first:
setup.confirmInstallWithShellRc: Install `ccp`/`claude` wrappers into {{binDir}} and update shell config {{shellRc}}. Continue?
setup.profileDesc: Create a Claude profile and fill in the upstream egress proxy it should actually use for outbound traffic.
setup.activeDesc: Choose an existing profile to become the active protection profile.
setup.proxyTitle: Active profile {{name}} has no upstream proxy
setup.proxyDesc: This is the upstream proxy Claude/CCP should use for real outbound traffic, not the local traffic-capture listening port.
setup.proxyOptionalDesc: Transparent capture is available, so a missing upstream proxy no longer blocks local traffic capture. Only configure it if you explicitly need CCP to own the outbound proxy chain.
setup.proxyHelp: Examples: `http://127.0.0.1:7890`, `http://host:port`, `socks5://user:pass@host:port`. If this stays empty, proxy injection and traffic capture stay inactive.
setup.proxyOptionalHelp: Transparent capture hooks local process traffic directly and does not depend on HTTP_PROXY. You can leave this empty for now unless you need the explicit proxy backend or a fixed upstream exit path later.
setup.confirmSaveProxy: Save a new proxy URL for profile {{name}}?
setup.proxySavedSuccess: Saved the upstream proxy for profile {{name}}.
setup.mitmTitle: HTTPS body capture is not ready
setup.mitmDesc: MITM certificates have not been prepared yet, so HTTPS interception cannot start even if traffic capture is running.
setup.confirmPrepareMitm: Generate MITM capture certificates in the current CCP state directory?
setup.prepareMitmSuccess: MITM capture certificates are ready. You can now install system trust or launch Claude to verify capture.
setup.mitmTrustTitle: System Trust For Other Local Clients
setup.mitmTrustDesc: Claude launched through CCP already trusts the local MITM root through NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS. Install system trust only if you want browsers or other native clients to trust it too.
setup.mitmTrustAutoPrepare: If MITM certificates are still missing, this button will prepare them first and then install system trust.
setup.confirmInstallMitmTrust: Install the CCP MITM root into the macOS login keychain?
setup.confirmRemoveMitmTrust: Remove the CCP MITM root from the macOS login keychain?
setup.transparentDesc: The selected capture mode wants mitmproxy local capture, but transparent capture is not ready on this machine yet.
setup.transparentHelp: Install mitmproxy/mitmdump if missing. On macOS, if Mitmproxy Redirector is waiting for approval, allow it in System Settings before retrying, or switch the capture backend back to explicit proxy mode.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.noToolsRunning: No proxied tools running. Start claude from terminal to enable protection.

profiles.name: Profile Name
profiles.empty: No profiles yet. Click "New Profile" to create one.

traffic.title: Traffic Capture
traffic.proxyHint: Capture proxy running at 127.0.0.1:{{port}}. New sessions launched through CCP or the installed `claude` wrapper will attach automatically; direct runs can still use HTTPS_PROXY=http://127.0.0.1:{{port}} manually.
traffic.transparentHint: Transparent capture is active and will try to intercept local traffic for process selector {{target}} without relying on HTTP_PROXY. New matching processes should be captured automatically while mitmdump remains healthy.
traffic.mitmScope: HTTPS body capture now terminates TLS locally and prefers decoding both HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2. Streaming responses update in place; non-HTTP TLS payloads or certificate-pinned connections still fall back to CONNECT metadata only.
traffic.startCaptureHint: Click "Start Capture" to begin recording traffic

detail.blockedDefault: This request targets a domain on the telemetry blocklist and was intercepted by DNS guard. No data left the machine.
detail.privacyDesc: Comparison of privacy-related fields before and after protection:
detail.fieldsReplaced: {{count}} privacy fields replaced in this request

settings.title: Settings
settings.captureMemoryDesc: Max memory for traffic capture data
settings.captureBackendDesc: auto prefers transparent capture and falls back to explicit proxy, transparent requires mitmproxy, explicit always uses the local proxy port.
